title: picrasma quassioides f. glabrescens
intro: Picrasma quassioides f. glabrescens, also known as the glabrous Quassia or Quassia amara, is a plant species that belongs to the family Simaroubaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant's bark and roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
